notes: Country-level VIZA RAG seed for Russia Unified e-Visa, aligned with the RU_E_VISA form seed. Sources reviewed: Russian MFA Consular Department unified e-visa landing page, instruction page, FAQ, and official electronic visa service.

chunks.title: Russia unified e-visa scope
chunks.content: The Russian MFA electronic visa service states that citizens of eligible foreign states can obtain a unified electronic visa for entry to the Russian Federation through approved border checkpoints. The e-visa is generally used for short visits such as tourism, business, humanitarian, cultural, scientific, sporting and similar purposes where eligible. It is not a work, residence, or long-term study route. VIZA should ask for nationality when eligibility is uncertain.

chunks.title: Russia unified e-visa conditions
chunks.content: The official e-visa service explains that a unified e-visa is issued through the MFA website or mobile application and must be used through approved Russian Federation checkpoints. Official pages describe the e-visa as a short-stay authorization and instruct travellers to check the rules, eligible citizenship, checkpoints, passport validity, and permitted stay before travel. VIZA should not recommend the e-visa to users who are not from eligible nationalities or who need work, residence, or a regular consular visa.

chunks.title: Russia e-visa online application process
chunks.content: The official Russian MFA e-visa process is online: fill in the application, pay the visa fee by bank card, receive notice of e-visa issuance on the website or by email, print or save the notification, and show it to the carrier and Russian border service at an approved checkpoint. The official site says e-visas are issued within not more than 4 calendar days. VIZA should still mark timing and fee details as official-page dependent.

chunks.title: Russia e-visa inputs and review
chunks.content: Russia unified e-visa preparation should collect identity and passport details, nationality, travel purpose, planned dates, accommodation or host information, contact details, employment or study status, prior Russia travel, background declarations, digital photo, passport bio-page data, and medical insurance details where required. The official service may return applications for correction, including photo issues, so VIZA should flag photo and passport accuracy before handoff.

chunks.title: Russia supported VIZA form route
chunks.content: VIZA supports Russia Unified e-Visa as country russia and visaType RU_E_VISA. When the user asks to apply for a Russia e-visa visitor route, send an application_redirect block to /client/application?country=russia&visaType=RU_E_VISA. Do not collect detailed application fields inside chat.

chunks.title: Russia fields and document preparation
chunks.content: The RU_E_VISA form schema collects applicant identity, other names, nationality, passport and other travel documents, contact and residence details, Russia travel purpose, itinerary, accommodation or host, prior Russia visits, visa refusals, criminal or deportation declarations, employment or study details, and document preparation such as passport bio page, compliant digital photo, medical insurance where applicable, and official e-visa account or application references.

chunks.title: Standard passport identity-field answer rules
chunks.content: Standard-answer identity fields must be answered from the applicant's passport biodata page, MRZ, official identity document, or the official dropdown options. This applies to passport number, name, date of birth, sex, nationality, issuing country, issue date, expiry date, issuing authority, place of issue, and passport/document type. For issuing authority or place-of-issue questions, do not infer the answer from the pickup city, residence city, application country, or travel destination; copy the exact Authority / Issuing authority / Place of issue wording printed on the passport if present. For Chinese ordinary passports, newer valid passports may show National Immigration Administration, PRC / 中华人民共和国国家移民管理局; older valid passports may show MPS Exit & Entry Administration / 公安部出入境管理局. If the passport prints a different authority, use the passport text. For passport type, select Ordinary / Regular / Normal only for ordinary personal passports; select Diplomatic, Official, Service, Special, Travel document, Refugee, or Other only when the document explicitly indicates that type. Official sources reviewed for this standard field rule include China National Immigration Administration certificate-issuing-authority notices and the Passport Law of the People's Republic of China; the passport biodata page remains the final source for the applicant's individual answer.

chunks.title: Official-source field answer norms
chunks.content:
Official-source field answer norms for russia.
Use these norms only as filling guidance; if a live official form, passport, or uploaded document shows a more specific value, that document controls the answer.

Crawled source URLs: https://electronic-visa.kdmid.ru/index_en.html ; https://evisa.kdmid.ru/Home/Instruction

Passport and identity fields:
- If you have a valid Russian visa in your passport, you do not need to apply for a unified e-visa.
- If your full date of birth is not specified in your passport, the unified e-visa application cannot be accepted for processing.
- Your passport must be machine-readable and issued by your country of nationality.

Issue, expiry, and validity fields:
- The unified e-visa is valid for the entry into and exit from the Russian Federation only through the following state border crossing points of the Russian Federation, the list of which is approved by the Government of the Russian Federation.
- The validity period of a unified electronic visa is 120 days from the date of issue.
- The permitted period of stay in the Russian Federation on the basis of a unified e-visa within its validity period shall not exceed 30 days from the date of entry.

Travel plan and port fields:
- granted an e-visa to enter the Russian Federation through the border crossing points .
- The unified e-visa is valid for the entry into and exit from the Russian Federation only through the following state border crossing points of the Russian Federation, the list of which is approved by the Government of the Russian Federation.
- The permitted period of stay in the Russian Federation on the basis of a unified e-visa within its validity period shall not exceed 30 days from the date of entry.

Accommodation, host, and address fields:
- E-visa applicants are not required to submit an invitation letter, hotel booking confirmation or any other document confirming the purpose of their travel to the Russian Federation.

Purpose, occupation, work, and study fields:
- E-visa applicants are not required to submit an invitation letter, hotel booking confirmation or any other document confirming the purpose of their travel to the Russian Federation.
- The unified e-visa entitles you to enter the Russian Federation and stay in the Russian Federation for private or business visit, tourism, as well as for participating in scientific, cultural, socio-political, economic, sporting events and carrying out relevant communications and contacts.
- If the purpose of your trip to the Russian Federation is none of the above, you have to apply to a diplomatic mission or consular post of the Russian Federation for a regular (not electronic) visa.

Funds, insurance, photo, and supporting-document fields:
- Foreign citizens staying in the Russian Federation on the basis of unified e-visas are required to have a medical insurance valid in the territory of the Russian Federation for the entire duration of their stay, with the exception of citizens of the following foreign countries who are exempt from this requirement on the basis of reciprocity.
- Digital photo of your passport data in JPEG format.
- Digital photo of your face in JPEG format that meets the requirements .

Answering rule: for closed-list fields, prefer the exact official option label/value. For document-backed fields, copy the passport, official identity document, application account, itinerary, invitation, accommodation, insurance, funds, or uploaded support document rather than guessing from a city, country, or prior answer.
